preamble: Assibilation of [d] to [dz] occurs when the stop develops a fricative release, often before high vowels.

examples.note: Historical Japanese shift of /d/ to /dz/ (later /z/) before /u/.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
